skill.name: find-outlook-emails
skill.description: Search Microsoft Outlook email through Rarefriend MCP and combine email results with contact history, CRM notes, and relationship context.
skill.summary: Find Outlook emails and view relevant Rarefriend contact context in one assistant workflow.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. Review shows most high-severity hits are false positives from setup examples, but the skill legitimately asks users to run an external Rarefriend MCP package, store client credentials, and connect Microsoft Outlook email, so it should publish only with a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Server Installation and Execution
medium_findings.description: The setup instructs users to run the Rarefriend MCP server with npx and configure it in assistant clients. This is a real external code execution risk because users execute a community npm package with access to OAuth credentials, even though the behavior is disclosed and required for the integration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and client configuration are explicit and directly launch @rarefriend-ai/mcp. The risk is elevated but the context is documented setup, not hidden execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Access to Microsoft Outlook Email and CRM Context
medium_findings.description: The skill routes Outlook email search and contact context through Rarefriend MCP. This creates privacy risk because email content, sender details, contact records, and notes may be exposed to a third-party service during normal use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly connects Microsoft email and searches inbox data through Rarefriend tools. This is intended functionality, but it handles sensitive user data.

medium_findings.title: OAuth Client Secret Stored in Local Assistant Configuration
medium_findings.description: The setup asks users to place RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET in local MCP environment configuration. This is common for MCP setup but exposes a credential if local config files, logs, or screenshots are shared.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential names and setup locations are explicit. The finding is medium because this is expected configuration rather than evidence of secret exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hidden Configuration File Static Hits Are Expected Client Setup
low_findings.description: The hidden-file findings point to Cursor and Claude Desktop configuration paths. These are standard MCP client configuration files, so the filesystem access pattern is disclosed setup guidance rather than covert file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are shown as user-edited configuration examples. No evidence found that the skill reads or writes hidden files itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections correspond to OAuth client ID and client secret wording, not to MD5, SHA-1, DES, or custom cryptographic code. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain credential labels and environment variables only. There is no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection in the skill files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Setup-State Checks
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections map to checking whether Rarefriend MCP and Microsoft email integration are connected. This is limited integration-state discovery, not broad host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands check connected integrations and sync status inside the Rarefriend tool domain. No evidence found of host inventory, port scanning, or environment harvesting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs External npm MCP Package
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented setup uses npx to run @rarefriend-ai/mcp. Users should verify the package source and understand that it receives configured Rarefriend credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The package invocation is explicit in both setup documentation and the main skill file. The risk depends on trusting the external package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Stores Service Credentials in MCP Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup places RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_ID and RAREFRIEND_CLIENT_SECRET in local assistant configuration. This pattern requires careful local file permissions and secret handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable names are present in concrete setup examples. This is a known secret-management concern, not a hidden data flow.

content.user_title: Search Outlook Emails with Contact Context
content.value_statement: Finding a specific Outlook email can be slow when relationship history lives elsewhere. This skill searches Outlook through Rarefriend and connects results to contact notes and CRM context.
actual_capabilities: Lists recent Microsoft Outlook emails with optional date and folder filters.
Searches Outlook emails by subject, body snippet, sender name, or sender email.
Checks whether Rarefriend MCP and Microsoft email integration are connected.
Starts the Microsoft email connection flow through Rarefriend when needed.
Cross-references email senders with Rarefriend contacts and relationship notes.
Can create a Rarefriend note after reviewing an email thread.
limitations: Requires a Rarefriend account and Rarefriend MCP credentials.
Requires Microsoft Outlook or Office 365 email connection approval.
Search results depend on Rarefriend sync status and available email permissions.
Does not operate as a standalone Outlook client without the MCP server.

use_cases.title: Find recent client replies
use_cases.description: Search a client name or email address, then review the latest Outlook messages and related contact history.

use_cases.title: Prepare for relationship follow-up
use_cases.description: Combine email threads with Rarefriend notes before drafting a follow-up or meeting update.

use_cases.title: Connect Outlook to assistant workflows
use_cases.description: Guide setup for Rarefriend MCP and Microsoft email, then confirm that sync is ready for search.

prompt_templates.title: Show recent inbox emails
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my last 10 Outlook emails and include sender, subject, date, and a short summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick inbox overview.

prompt_templates.title: Find email from a person
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Outlook emails from Jordan Lee this month. Then show any Rarefriend contact notes for Jordan.
prompt_templates.scenario: Search by sender and add relationship context.

prompt_templates.title: Research a topic across email
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Outlook emails about contract renewal from the last 90 days. Group results by sender and highlight open questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Analyze a topic across multiple messages.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a relationship brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent emails with Acme Corp, cross-reference Rarefriend contact history, and create a concise briefing for my next follow-up.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow that combines email search, CRM context, and synthesis.

output: A dated list of recent messages with sender, subject, and short summaries.
A note if the Microsoft email integration is not connected or still syncing.

output_examples.input: Find emails from Taylor about the proposal.
output: Matching messages grouped by conversation or date.
Relevant Rarefriend contact notes that help explain the relationship history.

output_examples.input: What did we last discuss with Acme?
output: A brief timeline of recent Outlook messages and related CRM notes.
Suggested follow-up points based on the conversation history.

best_practices: Confirm the Rarefriend MCP server is connected before starting email searches.
Use specific sender names, email addresses, dates, or topics to reduce noisy results.
Review sensitive email results before asking the assistant to create new CRM notes.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Rarefriend client secrets into chat messages or shared documents.
Do not use broad searches when a specific sender, date range, or topic is known.
Do not assume sync is complete until the integration status confirms it is ready.

faq.question: Does this skill work without Rarefriend MCP?
faq.answer: No. The skill depends on Rarefriend MCP tools for Outlook email search and contact context.

faq.question: Can it connect a Microsoft Outlook account?
faq.answer: Yes. It can start the Rarefriend Microsoft email connection flow and provide the browser link returned by the tool.

faq.question: What email fields can it search?
faq.answer: It can search subject, body snippet, sender name, and sender email when those fields are available through Rarefriend.

faq.question: Can it summarize relationship history?
faq.answer: Yes. It can combine email results with Rarefriend contact profiles and notes when matching contacts are found.

faq.question: Where are credentials configured?
faq.answer: Rarefriend client ID and client secret are configured in the local MCP client environment settings.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive inboxes?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill requires third-party email integration and should only be used with accounts approved for Rarefriend access.
